task_completion.description: Construct at least 3 interior partition walls and place at least 3 doors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan or 3D view in Sweet Home 3D shows at least 3 new interior walls dividing the space and at least 3 doors placed within those or existing walls.

task_completion.description: Furnish the judge's bench area with a desk, executive chair, and shelves/cabinets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct area (likely at the front of the room) containing at least 1 desk, 1 executive/office chair, and at least 2 shelving units or cabinets.

task_completion.description: Furnish the jury box and public gallery with at least 22 chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large number of chairs (at least 22 in total) arranged in rows for a public gallery and/or grouped together for a jury box.

task_completion.description: Furnish areas for counsel, witness, and clerk with tables/desks and chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Additional tables or desks (at least 3) and chairs (at least 7) arranged in the middle area for counsel, a witness stand, and a clerk's station.

task_completion.description: Furnish the courtroom with at least 35 items total from the catalog.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The furniture list panel on the left side of the Sweet Home 3D interface is heavily populated, or the 2D/3D views show a densely furnished room with at least 35 distinct items.

task_completion.description: Identify at least 4 zones using text labels on the floor plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D floor plan contains at least 4 text labels identifying different areas (e.g., 'Judge', 'Jury', 'Gallery', 'Counsel').
The Sweet Home 3D window title bar shows 'courtroom.sh3d', or a file manager/terminal window shows the file 'courtroom.sh3d' successfully saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to design the courtroom.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Sweet Home 3D application open, with the agent actively using the mouse/interface to draw walls and drag-and-drop furniture, rather than using terminal commands to modify XML files.

integrity.description: The 3D view consistently reflects the 2D floor plan modifications.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D view pane at the bottom of the Sweet Home 3D interface updates to show the walls, doors, and furniture placed in the 2D plan, proving genuine application usage.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a script or text editor to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim) or terminals running Python/Bash scripts to directly generate or manipulate the .sh3d archive are visible as the primary method of task completion.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying a starter file in Sweet Home 3D. The VLM must verify the presence of at least 3 new interior walls, 3 doors, and at least 35 total furniture items. Specific zone requirements include: Judge's area (min 1 desk, 1 executive chair, 2 shelves/cabinets), Jury/Gallery (min 22 chairs total), and Counsel/Witness/Clerk (min 3 tables/desks, 7 chairs total). At least 4 text labels must be present on the 2D floor plan. The final file must be saved as 'courtroom.sh3d'.
